feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: As cyclosporiasis cases rise, consumers are left guessing which foods to avoid
entry.summary: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

entry.title: Ebola is spreading faster in eastern Congo than it can be tracked, as deaths pass 700
entry.summary: Eighty percent of new Ebola cases in eastern Congo are emerging from unknown chains of transmission, according to WHO, a sign the outbreak is spreading faster than health officials can track.

entry.title: Study shows a blood test can help identify healthy people at high risk for Alzheimer's disease
entry.summary: A blood test that helps diagnose Alzheimer's might also forecast if healthy older adults will develop symptoms. A new study links high levels of so-called p-tau217 to a high risk of cognitive impairment in five or 10 years. Scientists stressed it's too soon t…

entry.title: Hamilton County health officials report more cyclosporiasis cases
entry.summary: As of noon Monday, Hamilton County Public Health is reporting 16 total cases, up from the 13 cases reported late last week.

entry.title: I smoked cannabis for nearly 20 years. Here’s what I wish I knew at 13.
entry.summary: As cannabis use becomes more widespread, we should be clear-eyed about some of the potential long-term effects.

entry.title: Salad greens, lettuce ‘possible source’ of cyclosporiasis parasite: Officials
entry.summary: Currently, Michigan and New York have the most cases of cyclosporiasis.

entry.title: 76 UES Buildings Test Positive For Legionella: See New List
entry.summary: Several private schools, including Spence and Dalton, are on the new list, as well as the Metropolitan Museum of Art.

entry.title: Investigation launched into extremely rare cancer striking kids in CA enclave: ‘Deeply concerning’
entry.summary: Bond Legal said it is confidentially gathering information from families in the area.

entry.title: As Cases of ‘Diarrhea Parasite’ Cyclospora Rise Across the US, Is It Safe to Eat Fresh Produce?
entry.summary: What foods / produce are safe amid a national increase in cyclosporiasis, the explosive diarrhea parasite? Experts on how cyclospora spreads, ways to stay safe.

entry.title: WHO warns DR Congo Ebola outbreak may be double the official tally
entry.summary: UN health agency says cases are underreported while doctors at outbreak epicentre threaten to go on strike.

entry.title: Your coffee habit may be making your liver healthier
entry.summary: A new study adds to the growing evidence that coffee is good for you.

entry.title: A data-driven framework reconstructs the molecular continuum of human MASLD progression
entry.summary: A trajectory-based molecular framework reveals coordinated temporal shifts in metabolic, inflammatory and fibrogenic mechanisms across the human MASLD spectrum.

entry.title: Column | How ‘smell training’ may improve cognition
entry.summary: Smell loss can be an early sign of neurodegenerative diseases, but it’s possible to boost your sniffing abilities.

entry.title: Why the fastest-growing Ebola outbreak in history is becoming more challenging
entry.summary: The Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo is now the fastest-growing one in history and new challenges keep emerging. A strike this week by unpaid workers at an Ebola treatment center could spark further unrest, even as a study begins of two badly needed treatments…

entry.title: Concerned you have bone thinning? Here’s what experts recommend.
entry.summary: You can avoid a life-altering fracture in the future by adjusting your lifestyle now.

entry.title: Exclusive | I had to push my organs back inside my body on a regular basis — but doctors said nothing was wrong
entry.summary: “I could maneuver it,” she said. “I can push it out. I can flip it to the side. It was just crazy.”

entry.title: A student claimed to have a Ph.D. in at least eight letters to journals. Two have been retracted.
entry.summary: This past year, Zhihao Lei has signed his name to at least 35 letters to the editor of medical journals, weighing in on topics from ICU care to breast cancer. In eight of them, he identified himsel…

entry.title: My Husband Has a Specific Arguing Style. I Don’t Like That He’s Using It With Our Kids.
entry.summary: How can we get through this?

entry.title: The bugs are getting worse
entry.summary: Because of climate, weather, acorn abundance (yes, really) and decisions about land use dating to colonial times, the bugs are indeed getting worse.

entry.title: Hours each day in an iron lung kept her breathing, her will to live kept her alive
entry.summary: The sister of the last known polio patient in the US to use the life-saving machine spoke to the BBC about her sister's life.

entry.title: Hours each day in an iron lung kept her breathing, her will to live kept her alive
entry.summary: The sister of the last known polio patient in the US to use the life-saving machine speaks to the BBC about her sister's life.

entry.title: Parasitic outbreak of cyclosporiasis linked in Michigan to lettuce
entry.summary: Michigan health officials said they have not yet pinpointed the grower or supplier where the outbreak originated.

entry.title: Three measles exposure locations identified in Oregon, OHA urging public to speak to health care provider
entry.summary: PORTLAND, Ore. (KTVZ) -- The Oregon Health Authority announced Monday, the department has identified three possible locations where members of the public could have been exposed to the measles. You can read the OHA's entire press release below, which includes…

entry.title: ADHD and periods: Are my hormones making my symptoms worse?
entry.summary: A first-of-its-kind study by UK researchers is looking at the link between menstrual cycles and ADHD.

entry.title: US continues to shun Ebola-infected citizens; second American sent to Germany
entry.summary: The man is said to be doing well in a Frankfurt hospital.

entry.title: The best electrolyte drinks for diarrhea as cyclosporiasis cases rise
entry.summary: Dealing with diarrhea during a cyclosporiasis outbreak? Doctors recommend drinks like Pedialyte and CeraLyte to restore fluids and electrolytes.

entry.title: The rare and dangerous heart condition tied to Lindsey Graham's death
entry.summary: Lindsey Graham reportedly died from an aortic dissection, which strikes without warning.

entry.title: Your Olive Oil Might Be Going Rancid in the Pantry—A Food Scientist Explains Why
entry.summary: A food scientist explains why olive oil goes rancid, the common storage mistakes that speed it up and why a light-blocking ceramic cruet can help keep it fresher longer.

entry.title: After My Wife Died, I Found A 4-Word Text Message In Her Phone That Hit Me Like A Sledgehammer
entry.summary: "She was the love of my life. We were together — willingly — for 36 years."

entry.title: Experts Warn Of Commonly Dismissed Dementia Symptoms That Can Surface 15 Years Before Doctors Catch It
entry.summary: “That’s scary,” a netizen wrote about the findings.

entry.title: An active 45-year-old man couldn't sit or use the bathroom without pain. He was diagnosed with stage 3 colon cancer.
entry.summary: Randy League was diagnosed with stage 3 colorectal cancer at 45 after feeling a sudden, sharp pain in his rectum.

entry.title: Testosterone Drives Early Pubertal Distress in Girls
entry.summary: A new study identifies testosterone as a major driver of emotional distress in pubertal girls.

entry.title: The surprising health benefit of being bald
entry.summary: Despite societal pressure to have a full head of hair, embracing baldness could prove beneficial when it comes to preventing a deadly cancer.

entry.title: Researchers link Long COVID to another virus
entry.summary: Researchers in Japan have released findings showing that COVID-19 after effects, including fatigue, occur when a coronavirus infection activates another virus already inside the body.

entry.title: Research Says This Skincare Step Is The Most Important For Fighting Wrinkles—And Most People Skip It
entry.summary: New research suggests that mineral sunscreen is one of the most important skincare products for fighting fine lines and wrinkles. Here's why.

entry.title: Latest NYC Legionnaires’ disease outbreak rises to 59 cases — with bacteria now detected for first time across park, at posh UWS building
entry.summary: No deaths have been reported in the latest local outbreak — which even ensnared the famed Guggenheim Museum — but more people are expected to test positive in the coming days, officials…

entry.title: Scientists Created a Wearable You Can Paint Directly Onto Your Skin
entry.summary: Researchers created a powerfully adhesive paintable electrode—and it’s over ten times more breathable in hot weather than normal medical electrodes.

entry.title: Scientists Reveal Humans Are Smart to Skip Cannibalism
entry.summary: Populations that practiced the taboo were prone to brain disease, study shows

entry.title: This Is the Only Brain Game That Actually Lowers Dementia Risk, Study Finds
entry.summary: New research breaks down the potential impact of playing a free online brain game. Here's what experts say about it.

entry.title: The Best After-Dinner Drink for Better Blood Sugar, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Discover why unsweetened decaf green tea is the best after-dinner drink for blood sugar, with dietitian-backed tips for supporting healthy glucose levels.

entry.title: Dementia study replicates promising outcomes following risk-reduction strategies
entry.summary: A dementia prevention trial praised as a "landmark" effort further demonstrated the promise of structured programs tailored to local cultures.

entry.title: Dozens at an Ebola treatment center in Congo strike over unpaid salaries and bonuses
entry.summary: Dozens of workers at an Ebola treatment center in northeast Congo have gone on strike over unpaid salaries and bonuses. The staff at Rwampara General Hospital in Ituri province includes epidemiologists, case investigators, drivers, and gravediggers. They say …

entry.title: This Everyday Drink Might Supercharge Weight Loss in a Surprising Way, Research Finds
entry.summary: A study suggests that sparkling—a.k.a. carbonated—water could "support weight loss." Here's what a personal trainer and dietitian want you to know.

entry.title: The 7 Best Anti-Inflammatory Ingredients to Add to Your Salad, According to Research
entry.summary: Chronic inflammation may increase the risk of diseases. Discover 7 anti-inflammatory foods to add to your salad to help you keep it at bay.

entry.title: Cancer rates are expected to nearly double in the next 24 years
entry.summary: Despite major progress in some areas — including decreased tobacco use that’s associated with 16 types of cancer — cancer is still a leading cause of death and disability.

entry.title: East End salmonella outbreak being investigated by Suffolk County health officials
entry.summary: Dozens of people have been hospitalized with the infection since the beginning of July.

entry.title: Woman Gets Life-Changing Breast Reduction From Size 36G to 36C
entry.summary: Ellen Larson was 24 when she first started considering breast reduction. Now 31 and the founder of jewelry brand Darling Devotion, Larson noticed that her ba...

entry.title: Woman Gets Life-Changing Breast Reduction From Size ’36G to 36C’
entry.summary: Ellen spent years convincing herself that she did not need the procedure. Now, she’s overjoyed that she finally did.

entry.title: Eating this food regularly could lead to greater longevity
entry.summary: I’ve heard spicy food can be good for you. Is that true? Here’s great news for people who love to feel the burn: The purported benefits of spicy foods have a...

entry.title: 5 signs you’re aging well — and the red flags to watch for in your 40s and 50s
entry.summary: While tech entrepreneurs and biohackers race to rewrite the rules of longevity, there are everyday telltale signs you’re aging well without a health tracker or blood tests.
